sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页主要讲 Overview：Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that reads your codebase, edits files, runs commands, and integrates with your development tools. Available in your terminal, IDE, desktop app, and browser.
你可以把它当成“Getting started”这块里专门管这一摊事的说明书。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把“Overview”理解成 Getting started 这一栏里的一把专门工具。这页不是让你背书，而是教你什么时候该把这把工具拿出来。
原文这页大多会按 Get started、What you can do、Use Claude Code everywhere、Next steps 这些环节往下讲。
翻成人话，大概就是：Use Claude Code everywhere

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 第一，先别一上来全开全配。先按最小一步试通，确认没跑偏，再继续往下加。
第二，命令、配置名、参数名这些硬东西尽量保留原样。人话解释是帮你听懂，不是帮你改关键字。
第三，照着原文这几个环节挨个过：Get started -> What you can do -> Use Claude Code everywhere -> Next steps。像下地先看水路、再试机器、再正式开干，一步一步最稳。

# Automate translations in CI
claude -p "translate new strings into French and raise a PR for review"

# Bulk operations across files
git diff main --name-only | claude -p "review these changed files for security issues"

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把“Changelog”理解成 Getting started 这一栏里的一把专门工具。这页不是让你背书，而是教你什么时候该把这把工具拿出来。
翻成人话，大概就是：Release notes for Claude Code, including new features, improvements, and bug fixes by version.

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 第一，先别一上来全开全配。先按最小一步试通，确认没跑偏，再继续往下加。
第二，命令、配置名、参数名这些硬东西尽量保留原样。人话解释是帮你听懂，不是帮你改关键字。
第三，照着原文这几个环节挨个过：2.1.77 -> 2.1.76 -> 2.1.75 -> 2.1.74。像下地先看水路、再试机器、再正式开干，一步一步最稳。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 Claude Code 到底是按什么路数干活的。
不是瞎猜瞎改，而是先看、再想、再动手、再回头验。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把它想成一个肯先绕地一圈的老把式。它不是看见一处泥巴就乱铲，而是先把地形、水路、工具和活路摸清。
这一页最值钱的是把“代理循环”“模型”“工具”“能碰到什么材料”这些底层门道讲明白。
你越懂这页，后面就越知道什么时候让它先分析，什么时候再让它真改。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 遇到大活，先让它摸底和列计划，不要一上来就吩咐大改。
知道它能看什么、能动什么、能跑什么命令，后面提要求才不容易超边界。
如果它跑偏，多半不是它突然变笨，而是你交代得太散、太大、太混。

sections.title: The agentic loop
paragraphs: 这一节讲“The agentic loop”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：When you give Claude a task, it works through three phases: gather context, take action, and verify results. These phases blend together. Claude uses tools throughout, whether searching files to understand your code, editing to make changes, or running tests to check its work.；The loop adapts to what you ask. A question about your codebase might only need context gathering. A bug fix cycles through all three phases repeatedly. A refactor might involve extensive verification. Claude decides what each step requires based on what it learned from the previous step, chaining dozens of actions together and course-correcting along the way.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

The interface determines how you see and interact with Claude, but the underlying agentic loop is identical. See Use Claude Code everywhere for the full list.。

sections.title: Work with sessions
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Work with sessions”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Claude Code saves your conversation locally as you work. Each message, tool use, and result is stored, which enables rewinding, resuming, and forking sessions. Before Claude makes code changes, it also snapshots the affected files so you can revert if needed.；Sessions are independent. Each new session starts with a fresh context window, without the conversation history from previous sessions. Claude can persist learnings across sessions using auto memory, and you can add your own persistent instructions in CLAUDE.md.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Work across branches
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Work across branches”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Each Claude Code conversation is a session tied to your current directory. When you resume, you only see sessions from that directory.；Claude sees your current branch’s files. When you switch branches, Claude sees the new branch’s files, but your conversation history stays the same. Claude remembers what you discussed even after switching.。

sections.title: Undo changes with checkpoints
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Undo changes with checkpoints”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Every file edit is reversible. Before Claude edits any file, it snapshots the current contents. If something goes wrong, press Esc twice to rewind to a previous state, or ask Claude to undo.；Checkpoints are local to your session, separate from git. They only cover file changes. Actions that affect remote systems (databases, APIs, deployments) can’t be checkpointed, which is why Claude asks before running commands with external side effects.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 Claude Code 身上都能再挂哪些家伙事。
不是只会一个人单打独斗，它还能接规矩、接技能、接外部工具、接自动化。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像给拖拉机看挂载清单。原机能开，但挂上不同设备以后，能耕地、能播种、能拉货。
这一页最重要的是帮你分清：什么时候该用 `CLAUDE.md`，什么时候该上 skills、subagents、hooks、MCP、plugins。
它不是叫你全装，而是叫你别装错。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从眼前最缺的一项能力补，不要为了“功能全”一口气全挂上。
几个功能名字看着像，实际负责的事情不一样，这页就是拿来分清边界的。
加功能之前先想收益，再想复杂度，别把帮工越改越笨重。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Extensions plug into different parts of the agentic loop:；CLAUDE.md adds persistent context Claude sees every session；Skills add reusable knowledge and invocable workflows。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Match features to your goal
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Match features to your goal”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Features range from always-on context that Claude sees every session, to on-demand capabilities you or Claude can invoke, to background automation that runs on specific events. The table below shows what’s available and when each one makes sense.；Plugins are the packaging layer. A plugin bundles skills, hooks, subagents, and MCP servers into a single installable unit. Plugin skills are namespaced (like /my-plugin:review) so multiple plugins can coexist. Use plugins when you want to reuse the same setup across multiple repositories or distribute to others via a marketplace.。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

如果你打算把子能力往里挂，这里提到的 hooks、MCP、skills、memory 都要分清各自负责哪一摊。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：MCP servers override by name: local > project > user. See MCP scope.；Hooks merge: all registered hooks fire for their matching events regardless of source. See hooks.。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

sections.title: Combine features
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Combine features”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Each extension solves a different problem: CLAUDE.md handles always-on context, skills handle on-demand knowledge and workflows, MCP handles external connections, subagents handle isolation, and hooks handle automation. Real setups combine them based on your workflow.；For example, you might use CLAUDE.md for project conventions, a skill for your deployment workflow, MCP to connect to your database, and a hook to run linting after every edit. Each feature handles what it’s best at.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Understand context costs
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Understand context costs”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Every feature you add consumes some of Claude’s context. Too much can fill up your context window, but it can also add noise that makes Claude less effective; skills may not trigger correctly, or Claude may lose track of your conventions. Understanding these trade-offs helps you build an effective setup.。
如果你打算把子能力往里挂，这里提到的 hooks、MCP、skills、memory 都要分清各自负责哪一摊。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页主要讲 Store instructions and memories：Give Claude persistent instructions with CLAUDE.md files, and let Claude accumulate learnings automatically with auto memory.
你可以把它当成“Core concepts”这块里专门管这一摊事的说明书。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把“Store instructions and memories”理解成 Core concepts 这一栏里的一把专门工具。这页不是让你背书，而是教你什么时候该把这把工具拿出来。
原文这页大多会按 CLAUDE.md vs auto memory、CLAUDE.md files、Choose where to put CLAUDE.md files、Set up a project CLAUDE.md 这些环节往下讲。
翻成人话，大概就是：CLAUDE.md vs auto memory

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 第一，先别一上来全开全配。先按最小一步试通，确认没跑偏，再继续往下加。
第二，命令、配置名、参数名这些硬东西尽量保留原样。人话解释是帮你听懂，不是帮你改关键字。
第三，照着原文这几个环节挨个过：CLAUDE.md vs auto memory -> CLAUDE.md files -> Choose where to put CLAUDE.md files -> Set up a project CLAUDE.md。像下地先看水路、再试机器、再正式开干，一步一步最稳。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Import additional files 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：CLAUDE.md files can import additional files using @path/to/import syntax. Imported files are expanded and loaded into context at launch alongside the CLAUDE.md that references them. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
See @README for project overview and @package.json for available npm commands for this project.

# Additional Instructions
- git workflow @docs/git-instructions.md

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Import additional files 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：CLAUDE.md files can import additional files using @path/to/import syntax. Imported files are expanded and loaded into context at launch alongside the CLAUDE.md that references them. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：How CLAUDE.md files load
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md files by walking up the directory tree from your current working directory, checking each directory along the way. This means if you run Claude Code in foo/bar/, it loads instructions from both foo/bar/CLAUDE.md and foo/CLAUDE.md. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Organize rules with .claude/rules/ 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一小节原文主要在讲：For larger projects, you can organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. This keeps instructions modular and easier for teams to maintain. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths, so they only load into context when Claude works with matching files, reducing noise and saving context space. 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
your-project/
├── .claude/
│   ├── CLAUDE.md           # Main project instructions
│   └── rules/
│       ├── code-style.md   # Code style guidelines
│       ├── testing.md      # Testing conventions
│       └── security.md     # Security requirements

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Organize rules with .claude/rules/ 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：For larger projects, you can organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. This keeps instructions modular and easier for teams to maintain. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths, so they only load into context when Claude works with matching files, reducing noise and saving context space. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
paths:
  - "src/api/**/*.ts"
---

# API Development Rules

- All API endpoints must include input validation
- Use the standard error response format
- Include OpenAPI documentation comments

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Organize rules with .claude/rules/ 3
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：For larger projects, you can organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. This keeps instructions modular and easier for teams to maintain. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths, so they only load into context when Claude works with matching files, reducing noise and saving context space. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Manage CLAUDE.md for large teams
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：For organizations deploying Claude Code across teams, you can centralize instructions and control which CLAUDE.md files are loaded. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Enable or disable auto memory
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Auto memory is on by default. To toggle it, open /memory in a session and use the auto memory toggle, or set autoMemoryEnabled in your project settings: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Storage location 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Each project gets its own memory directory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/. The <project> path is derived from the git repository, so all worktrees and subdirectories within the same repo share one auto memory directory. Outside a git repo, the project root is used instead. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Storage location 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一小节原文主要在讲：Each project gets its own memory directory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/. The <project> path is derived from the git repository, so all worktrees and subdirectories within the same repo share one auto memory directory. Outside a git repo, the project root is used instead. 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/
├── MEMORY.md          # Concise index, loaded into every session
├── debugging.md       # Detailed notes on debugging patterns
├── api-conventions.md # API design decisions
└── ...                # Any other topic files Claude creates

sections.title: CLAUDE.md vs auto memory
paragraphs: 这一节讲“CLAUDE.md vs auto memory”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Subagents can also maintain their own auto memory. See subagent configuration for details.。
如果你打算把子能力往里挂，这里提到的 hooks、MCP、skills、memory 都要分清各自负责哪一摊。

sections.title: CLAUDE.md files
paragraphs: 这一节讲“CLAUDE.md files”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：CLAUDE.md files are markdown files that give Claude persistent instructions for a project, your personal workflow, or your entire organization. You write these files in plain text; Claude reads them at the start of every session.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Choose where to put CLAUDE.md files
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Choose where to put CLAUDE.md files”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：CLAUDE.md files can live in several locations, each with a different scope. More specific locations take precedence over broader ones.；CLAUDE.md files in the directory hierarchy above the working directory are loaded in full at launch. CLAUDE.md files in subdirectories load on demand when Claude reads files in those directories. See How CLAUDE.md files load for the full resolution order.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Set up a project CLAUDE.md
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Set up a project CLAUDE.md”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：A project CLAUDE.md can be stored in either ./CLAUDE.md or ./.claude/CLAUDE.md. Create this file and add instructions that apply to anyone working on the project: build and test commands, coding standards, architectural decisions, naming conventions, and common workflows. These instructions are shared with your team through version control, so focus on project-level standards rather than personal preferences.；Run /init to generate a starting CLAUDE.md automatically. Claude analyzes your codebase and creates a file with build commands, test instructions, and project conventions it discovers. If a CLAUDE.md already exists, /init suggests improvements rather than overwriting it. Refine from there with instructions Claude wouldn’t discover on its own.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Write effective instructions
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Write effective instructions”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Specificity: write instructions that are concrete enough to verify. For example:；“Use 2-space indentation” instead of “Format code properly”；“Run npm test before committing” instead of “Test your changes”。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

paragraphs: 这一节讲“Import additional files”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：To pull in a README, package.json, and a workflow guide, reference them with @ syntax anywhere in your CLAUDE.md:；For a more structured approach to organizing instructions, see .claude/rules/.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: Import additional files 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：CLAUDE.md files can import additional files using @path/to/import syntax. Imported files are expanded and loaded into context at launch alongside the CLAUDE.md that references them. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
See @README for project overview and @package.json for available npm commands for this project.

# Additional Instructions
- git workflow @docs/git-instructions.md

steps.title: Import additional files 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：CLAUDE.md files can import additional files using @path/to/import syntax. Imported files are expanded and loaded into context at launch alongside the CLAUDE.md that references them. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: How CLAUDE.md files load
paragraphs: 这一节讲“How CLAUDE.md files load”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：If you work in a large monorepo where other teams’ CLAUDE.md files get picked up, use claudeMdExcludes to skip them.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: How CLAUDE.md files load
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md files by walking up the directory tree from your current working directory, checking each directory along the way. This means if you run Claude Code in foo/bar/, it loads instructions from both foo/bar/CLAUDE.md and foo/CLAUDE.md. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Organize rules with .claude/rules/
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Organize rules with .claude/rules/”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Rules without paths frontmatter are loaded at launch with the same priority as .claude/CLAUDE.md.；Use glob patterns in the paths field to match files by extension, directory, or any combination:；You can specify multiple patterns and use brace expansion to match multiple extensions in one pattern:。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: Organize rules with .claude/rules/ 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一节原文主要在讲：For larger projects, you can organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. This keeps instructions modular and easier for teams to maintain. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths, so they only load into context when Claude works with matching files, reducing noise and saving context space. 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
your-project/
├── .claude/
│   ├── CLAUDE.md           # Main project instructions
│   └── rules/
│       ├── code-style.md   # Code style guidelines
│       ├── testing.md      # Testing conventions
│       └── security.md     # Security requirements

steps.title: Organize rules with .claude/rules/ 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：For larger projects, you can organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. This keeps instructions modular and easier for teams to maintain. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths, so they only load into context when Claude works with matching files, reducing noise and saving context space. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
paths:
  - "src/api/**/*.ts"
---

# API Development Rules

- All API endpoints must include input validation
- Use the standard error response format
- Include OpenAPI documentation comments

steps.title: Enable or disable auto memory
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Auto memory is on by default. To toggle it, open /memory in a session and use the auto memory toggle, or set autoMemoryEnabled in your project settings: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Storage location
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Storage location”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：To store auto memory in a different location, set autoMemoryDirectory in your user or local settings:；The directory contains a MEMORY.md entrypoint and optional topic files:。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: Storage location 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Each project gets its own memory directory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/. The <project> path is derived from the git repository, so all worktrees and subdirectories within the same repo share one auto memory directory. Outside a git repo, the project root is used instead. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Storage location 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一节原文主要在讲：Each project gets its own memory directory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/. The <project> path is derived from the git repository, so all worktrees and subdirectories within the same repo share one auto memory directory. Outside a git repo, the project root is used instead. 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/
├── MEMORY.md          # Concise index, loaded into every session
├── debugging.md       # Detailed notes on debugging patterns
├── api-conventions.md # API design decisions
└── ...                # Any other topic files Claude creates

sections.title: How it works
paragraphs: 这一节讲“How it works”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：The first 200 lines of MEMORY.md are loaded at the start of every conversation. Content beyond line 200 is not loaded at session start. Claude keeps MEMORY.md concise by moving detailed notes into separate topic files.；This 200-line limit applies only to MEMORY.md. CLAUDE.md files are loaded in full regardless of length, though shorter files produce better adherence.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Audit and edit your memory
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Audit and edit your memory”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Auto memory files are plain markdown you can edit or delete at any time. Run /memory to browse and open memory files from within a session.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: View and edit with /memory
paragraphs: 这一节讲“View and edit with /memory”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：The /memory command lists all CLAUDE.md and rules files loaded in your current session, lets you toggle auto memory on or off, and provides a link to open the auto memory folder. Select any file to open it in your editor.；When you ask Claude to remember something, like “always use pnpm, not npm” or “remember that the API tests require a local Redis instance,” Claude saves it to auto memory. To add instructions to CLAUDE.md instead, ask Claude directly, like “add this to CLAUDE.md,” or edit the file yourself via /memory.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Troubleshoot memory issues
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Troubleshoot memory issues”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：These are the most common issues with CLAUDE.md and auto memory, along with steps to debug them.。
如果你打算把子能力往里挂，这里提到的 hooks、MCP、skills、memory 都要分清各自负责哪一摊。

sections.title: Claude isn’t following my CLAUDE.md
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Claude isn’t following my CLAUDE.md”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：To debug:；Run /memory to verify your CLAUDE.md files are being loaded. If a file isn’t listed, Claude can’t see it.；Make instructions more specific. “Use 2-space indentation” works better than “format code nicely.”。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: I don’t know what auto memory saved
paragraphs: 这一节讲“I don’t know what auto memory saved”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Run /memory and select the auto memory folder to browse what Claude has saved. Everything is plain markdown you can read, edit, or delete.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: My CLAUDE.md is too large
paragraphs: 这一节讲“My CLAUDE.md is too large”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Files over 200 lines consume more context and may reduce adherence. Move detailed content into separate files referenced with @path imports (see Import additional files), or split your instructions across .claude/rules/ files.。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Core concepts 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲日常最常见的派活套路。
像看新项目、找相关代码、修 bug、重构、补测试，这些都给了顺手走法。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像老农教你干活的顺序：先看地，再找水，再下种，不是抡起锄头就全翻。
这一页特别适合新手，因为它不是讲抽象能力，而是把常见活拆成一步一步。
你照着这些套路派活，比自己临场乱说稳得多。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 新项目先看全貌，再找相关文件，不要一开始就陷进一个函数里。
修 bug 前先定位范围，再动手，再验结果，这个节奏别乱。
如果活很大，就拆成几段：摸底、实现、验证，各段分开看。

paragraphs: 这一节讲“Use extended thinking (thinking mode)”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Extended thinking is enabled by default, giving Claude space to reason through complex problems step-by-step before responding. This reasoning is visible in verbose mode, which you can toggle on with Ctrl+O.；Additionally, Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 support adaptive reasoning: instead of a fixed thinking token budget, the model dynamically allocates thinking based on your effort level setting. Extended thinking and adaptive reasoning work together to give you control over how deeply Claude reasons before responding.。
这里还牵扯模型怎么选，速度、成本和能力通常要一起掂量。

steps.title: Run parallel Claude Code sessions with Git worktrees 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：When working on multiple tasks at once, you need each Claude session to have its own copy of the codebase so changes don’t collide. Git worktrees solve this by creating separate working directories that each have their own files and branch, while sharing the same repository history and remote connections. This means you can have Claude working on a feature in one worktree while fixing a bug in another, without either session interfering with the other. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Start Claude in a worktree named "feature-auth"
# Creates .claude/worktrees/feature-auth/ with a new branch
claude --worktree feature-auth

# Start another session in a separate worktree
claude --worktree bugfix-123

steps.title: Run parallel Claude Code sessions with Git worktrees 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：When working on multiple tasks at once, you need each Claude session to have its own copy of the codebase so changes don’t collide. Git worktrees solve this by creating separate working directories that each have their own files and branch, while sharing the same repository history and remote connections. This means you can have Claude working on a feature in one worktree while fixing a bug in another, without either session interfering with the other. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

Ask Claude to “use worktrees for your agents” or configure it in a custom subagent by adding isolation: worktree to the agent’s frontmatter. Each subagent gets its own worktree that is automatically cleaned up when the subagent finishes without changes.。

steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：For more control over worktree location and branch configuration, create worktrees with Git directly. This is useful when you need to check out a specific existing branch or place the worktree outside the repository. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Create a worktree with a new branch
git worktree add ../project-feature-a -b feature-a

# Create a worktree with an existing branch
git worktree add ../project-bugfix bugfix-123

# Start Claude in the worktree
cd ../project-feature-a && claude

# Clean up when done
git worktree list
git worktree remove ../project-feature-a

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Core concepts 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲怎么把 Claude Code 使唤得更顺，不是讲新功能，是讲怎么少返工。
核心就一句话：话说清、边界画清、验收讲清。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 再好的帮工，也怕遇到一句“你帮我弄一下”的糊涂单。
这一页会教你怎么给它验证办法、怎么先探索再规划、怎么提供更具体的上下文和更有用的材料。
它不是花招，而是你以后少踩坑的基本功。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 别只说目标，也要说约束和验收方式。
给它丰富上下文时，宁可给关键材料，也别一股脑塞一车废话。
能让它自己验证的事，尽量给它验证条件，这比让它蒙着干靠谱得多。

name: api-conventions
description: REST API design conventions for our services
---
# API Conventions
- Use kebab-case for URL paths
- Use camelCase for JSON properties
- Always include pagination for list endpoints
- Version APIs in the URL path (/v1/, /v2/)

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create skills 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Create SKILL.md files in .claude/skills/ to give Claude domain knowledge and reusable workflows. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: fix-issue
description: Fix a GitHub issue
disable-model-invocation: true
---
Analyze and fix the GitHub issue: $ARGUMENTS.

1. Use `gh issue view` to get the issue details
2. Understand the problem described in the issue
3. Search the codebase for relevant files
4. Implement the necessary changes to fix the issue
5. Write and run tests to verify the fix
6. Ensure code passes linting and type checking
7. Create a descriptive commit message
8. Push and create a PR

steps.code:
---
name: security-reviewer
description: Reviews code for security vulnerabilities
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
model: opus
---
You are a senior security engineer. Review code for:
- Injection vulnerabilities (SQL, XSS, command injection)
- Authentication and authorization flaws
- Secrets or credentials in code
- Insecure data handling

Provide specific line references and suggested fixes.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Let Claude interview you
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：For larger features, have Claude interview you first. Start with a minimal prompt and ask Claude to interview you using the AskUserQuestion tool. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
I want to build [brief description]. Interview me in detail using the AskUserQuestion tool.

Ask about technical implementation, UI/UX, edge cases, concerns, and tradeoffs. Don't ask obvious questions, dig into the hard parts I might not have considered.

Keep interviewing until we've covered everything, then write a complete spec to SPEC.md.

I want to build [brief description]. Interview me in detail using the AskUserQuestion tool.

Ask about technical implementation, UI/UX, edge cases, concerns, and tradeoffs. Don't ask obvious questions, dig into the hard parts I might not have considered.

steps.title: Fan out across files 1

remote-control.sourceLabel: Continue local sessions from any device with Remote Control

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲远程控制，也就是人离开电脑了，还能继续盯着本机里的 Claude Code 干活。
手机、平板、浏览器都能接上来看。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像你人去镇上了，但院子里的帮工还在干活，你拿手机就能继续喊两句。
这一页会讲要满足哪些前提、怎么在本机开远程会话、怎么从别的设备接进来，以及安全边界。
如果你常常任务做到一半要离开电脑，这页很有用。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先在自己最熟的机器上开一次远程会话，确认接入链路跑通。
远程适合续命和盯进度，不适合第一次就在外面做高风险大改。
安全和连接限制要先看清，别只顾方便。

sections.title: Enable Remote Control for all sessions
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Enable Remote Control for all sessions”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：By default, Remote Control only activates when you explicitly run claude remote-control, claude --remote-control, or /remote-control. To enable it automatically for every interactive session, run /config inside Claude Code and set Enable Remote Control for all sessions to true. Set it back to false to disable.；With this setting on, each interactive Claude Code process registers one remote session. If you run multiple instances, each one gets its own environment and session. To run multiple concurrent sessions from a single process, use server mode with --spawn instead.。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

sections.title: Connection and security
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Connection and security”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Your local Claude Code session makes outbound HTTPS requests only and never opens inbound ports on your machine. When you start Remote Control, it registers with the Anthropic API and polls for work. When you connect from another device, the server routes messages between the web or mobile client and your local session over a streaming connection.；All traffic travels through the Anthropic API over TLS, the same transport security as any Claude Code session. The connection uses multiple short-lived credentials, each scoped to a single purpose and expiring independently.。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

sections.title: Remote Control vs Claude Code on the web
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Remote Control vs Claude Code on the web”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Remote Control and Claude Code on the web both use the claude.ai/code interface. The key difference is where the session runs: Remote Control executes on your machine, so your local MCP servers, tools, and project configuration stay available. Claude Code on the web executes in Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure.；Use Remote Control when you’re in the middle of local work and want to keep going from another device. Use Claude Code on the web when you want to kick off a task without any local setup, work on a repo you don’t have cloned, or run multiple tasks in parallel.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Limitations
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Limitations”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：One remote session per interactive process: outside of server mode, each Claude Code instance supports one remote session at a time. Use server mode with --spawn to run multiple concurrent sessions from a single process.；Terminal must stay open: Remote Control runs as a local process. If you close the terminal or stop the claude process, the session ends. Run claude remote-control again to start a new one.。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

sections.title: Related resources
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Related resources”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Claude Code on the web: run sessions in Anthropic-managed cloud environments instead of on your machine；Authentication: set up /login and manage credentials for claude.ai；CLI reference: full list of flags and commands including claude remote-control。
这里还牵扯模型怎么选，速度、成本和能力通常要一起掂量。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲网页版 Claude Code，也就是把任务放到云上跑，不必全靠你本地机器撑着。
适合异步长任务、跨设备续上工作。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像把活送去一个安全工棚里慢慢做，你人不一定守在旁边。
这一页会讲谁能用、怎么开始、网页版到底怎么运作、怎么在网页和终端之间来回接力。
如果你想把长任务丢出去跑，或者在外头也能接着看结果，这页特别值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先拿一个不太重但也不是太小的任务试试网页版节奏。
网页版和终端不是二选一，很多时候是配合着用。
如果任务和本地环境强绑定，还是先想清楚该不该搬到网页上。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：You can start new tasks on the web from your terminal, or pull web sessions into your terminal to continue locally. Web sessions persist even if you close your laptop, and you can monitor them from anywhere including the Claude mobile app.；Session handoff is one-way: you can pull web sessions into your terminal, but you can’t push an existing terminal session to the web. The --remote flag creates a new web session for your current repository.。

sections.title: Sharing sessions
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Sharing sessions”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：To share a session, toggle its visibility according to the account types below. After that, share the session link as-is. Recipients who open your shared session will see the latest state of the session upon load, but the recipient’s page will not update in real time.；For Enterprise and Teams accounts, the two visibility options are Private and Team. Team visibility makes the session visible to other members of your Claude.ai organization. Repository access verification is enabled by default, based on the GitHub account connected to the recipient’s account. Your account’s display name is visible to all recipients with access. Claude in Slack sessions are automatically shared with Team visibility.。

lines: 这是 Platforms and integrations 里的一摊要紧活

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 Chrome 扩展，让 Claude Code 能直接碰浏览器现场。
调网页、看控制台、自动点表单、抓页面数据，这类活都更顺。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像你不再只让帮工看图纸，而是直接带它去现场看墙有没有裂、门能不能开。
这一页会讲扩展能干什么、要准备什么、怎么从 CLI 启起来、怎么给站点权限。
如果你做前端、做网页排错，这页很实用。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从一个简单页面试，比如本地开发页或测试环境。
浏览器权限别图省事全开，按站点和需要一点点给。
适合网页现场问题，不适合拿来替代所有普通代码分析。

sections.title: Prerequisites

vs-code.sourceLabel: Use Claude Code in VS Code

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 VS Code 集成，把 Claude Code 直接请进你平时写代码的工位。
看 diff、提问、@ 文件、续上会话，都能在编辑器里干。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像把帮工请到你手边，不用你在编辑器和终端之间来回跑。
这一页会讲扩展怎么装、怎么开始、提示框怎么用、怎么引用文件和目录、怎么接回以前的会话。
如果你本来就长期待在 VS Code，这页大概率是你的主入口。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先把扩展装好，再拿一个小改动试从编辑器里直接派活。
善用文件引用和历史会话续接，不然它还是容易认路慢。
编辑器里适合边看边改，复杂脚本化操作还是终端更直接。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Reference files and folders
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use @-mentions to give Claude context about specific files or folders. When you type @ followed by a file or folder name, Claude reads that content and can answer questions about it or make changes to it. Claude Code supports fuzzy matching, so you can type partial names to find what you need: 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
> Explain the logic in @auth (fuzzy matches auth.js, AuthService.ts, etc.)
> What's in @src/components/ (include a trailing slash for folders)

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Automate browser tasks with Chrome
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Connect Claude to your Chrome browser to test web apps, debug with console logs, and automate browser workflows without leaving VS Code. This requires the Claude in Chrome extension version 1.0.36 or higher. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: @browser go to localhost:3000 and check the console for errors

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Connect to external tools with MCP
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers give Claude access to external tools, databases, and APIs. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: claude mcp add --transport http github https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create commits and pull requests
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：Claude can stage changes, write commit messages, and create pull requests based on your work: 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
> commit my changes with a descriptive message
> create a pr for this feature
> summarize the changes I've made to the auth module

steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use the --worktree (-w) flag to start Claude in an isolated worktree with its own files and branch: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

When your cursor is in a code file, the editor is focused. When your cursor is in Claude’s prompt box, Claude is focused. Use Cmd+Esc / Ctrl+Esc to toggle between them.。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Claude Code is available as both a VS Code extension (graphical panel) and a CLI (command-line interface in the terminal). Some features are only available in the CLI. If you need a CLI-only feature, run claude in VS Code’s integrated terminal.。

sections.title: Rewind with checkpoints

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Fork conversation from here: start a new conversation branch from this message while keeping all code changes intact；Fork conversation and rewind code: start a new conversation branch and revert file changes to this point；For full details on how checkpoints work and their limitations, see Checkpointing.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Switch between extension and CLI
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Switch between extension and CLI”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：The extension and CLI share the same conversation history. To continue an extension conversation in the CLI, run claude --resume in the terminal. This opens an interactive picker where you can search for and select your conversation.。

sections.title: Include terminal output in prompts
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Include terminal output in prompts”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Reference terminal output in your prompts using @terminal:name where name is the terminal’s title. This lets Claude see command output, error messages, or logs without copy-pasting.。

sections.title: Monitor background processes
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Monitor background processes”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：When Claude runs long-running commands, the extension shows progress in the status bar. However, visibility for background tasks is limited compared to the CLI. For better visibility, have Claude output the command so you can run it in VS Code’s integrated terminal.。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 JetBrains 系列 IDE 集成，适合 IntelliJ、PyCharm、WebStorm 这些用户。
目的和 VS Code 一样，就是别离开主力工位。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像把帮工请进你自己那套熟手工作台，不用你临时换桌子。
这一页会讲支持哪些 IDE、有哪些功能、怎么装、从 IDE 里怎么用、从外部终端怎么配合。
如果你本来就是 JetBrains 老用户，这页最能帮你少折腾。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先确认插件装好了、IDE 识别正常，再开始真派活。
越是大工程，越能体会在 IDE 里直接协作比来回切换省劲。
从 IDE 和从外部终端接入的差别最好先试一遍。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 Slack 集成，也就是在团队聊天里直接把活交给 Claude Code。
有人在群里报 bug、提需求、催调查时，不必再来回复制粘贴。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像在大队群里喊一声，帮工就顺着聊天内容去接单。
这一页会讲适合哪些用例、要准备什么、Slack 里怎么接起来、接进去后它怎么流转。
如果你们团队很多协作都在 Slack 里发生，这页很值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先拿一类固定场景试，比如报障或代码审查请求。
群里说的话也要尽量清楚，不然它接到的还是糊涂单。
适合把群消息变成任务，不代表群里每句话都该让它接。

这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

The full transcript is preserved and always accessible.；On the web: The complete Claude Code session with full conversation history, all code changes, file operations, and the ability to continue the session or create pull requests.。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: When to use Slack vs. web
paragraphs: 这一节讲“When to use Slack vs. web”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Use Slack when: Context already exists in a Slack discussion, you want to kick off a task asynchronously, or you’re collaborating with teammates who need visibility.；Use the web directly when: You need to upload files, want real-time interaction during development, or are working on longer, more complex tasks.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Sessions not starting
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Sessions not starting”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Verify your Claude account is connected in the Claude App Home；Check that you have Claude Code on the web access enabled；Ensure you have at least one GitHub repository connected to Claude Code。

sections.title: Repository not showing
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Repository not showing”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Connect the repository in Claude Code on the web at claude.ai/code；Verify your GitHub permissions for that repository；Try disconnecting and reconnecting your GitHub account。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Wrong repository selected
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Wrong repository selected”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Click the “Change Repo” button to select a different repository；Include the repository name in your request for more accurate selection。

sections.title: Authentication errors
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Authentication errors”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Disconnect and reconnect your Claude account in the App Home；Ensure you’re signed into the correct Claude account in your browser；Check that your Claude plan includes Claude Code access。

sections.title: Session expiration
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Session expiration”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Sessions remain accessible in your Claude Code history on the web；You can continue or reference past sessions from claude.ai/code。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

sections.title: Current limitations
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Current limitations”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Platforms and integrations 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲怎么养专门干单项活的小帮工。
一个查问题，一个写测试，一个整理文档，各干各的，脑子不打架。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 子代理就像把一个大工棚拆成几个小工位。不同活放到不同工位上干，现场更清爽。
这一页会讲内置子代理、怎么快速做第一个子代理、怎么配置、怎么用 `/agents` 管。
如果你发现一个会话里什么活都塞，越干越乱，这页就该上了。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从最容易切分的活开始，比如 reviewer、test-writer、docs-helper。
子代理描述一定要写清它负责什么，不负责什么。
作用域别乱放，项目级和个人级要分明，不然迟早互相打架。

name: code-reviewer
description: Reviews code for quality and best practices

model: sonnet

You are a code reviewer. When invoked, analyze the code and provide
specific, actionable feedback on quality, security, and best practices.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Define hooks for subagents 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Subagents can define hooks that run during the subagent’s lifecycle. There are two ways to configure hooks: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Review code changes with automatic linting
hooks:
  PreToolUse:
    - matcher: "Bash"
      hooks:
        - type: command
          command: "./scripts/validate-command.sh $TOOL_INPUT"
  PostToolUse:
    - matcher: "Edit|Write"
      hooks:
        - type: command
          command: "./scripts/run-linter.sh"
---

steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Claude automatically delegates tasks based on the task description in your request, the description field in subagent configurations, and current context. To encourage proactive delegation, include phrases like “use proactively” in your subagent’s description field. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Common patterns 1
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：One of the most effective uses for subagents is isolating operations that produce large amounts of output. Running tests, fetching documentation, or processing log files can consume significant context. By delegating these to a subagent, the verbose output stays in the subagent’s context while only the relevant summary returns to your main conversation. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

  - github
---

sections.title: Code reviewer
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Code reviewer”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：A read-only subagent that reviews code without modifying it. This example shows how to design a focused subagent with limited tool access (no Edit or Write) and a detailed prompt that specifies exactly what to look for and how to format output.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: Code reviewer
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：A read-only subagent that reviews code without modifying it. This example shows how to design a focused subagent with limited tool access (no Edit or Write) and a detailed prompt that specifies exactly what to look for and how to format output. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Expert code review specialist. Proactively reviews code for quality, security, and maintainability. Use immediately after writing or modifying code.
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
model: inherit
---

You are a senior code reviewer ensuring high standards of code quality and security.

When invoked:
1. Run git diff to see recent changes
2. Focus on modified files
3. Begin review immediately

Review checklist:
- Code is clear and readable
- Functions and variables are well-named
- No duplicated code
- Proper error handling
- No exposed secrets or API keys
- Input validation implemented
- Good test coverage
- Performance considerations addressed

Provide feedback organized by priority:
- Critical issues (must fix)
- Warnings (should fix)
- Suggestions (consider improving)

Include specific examples of how to fix issues.

sections.title: Debugger
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Debugger”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：A subagent that can both analyze and fix issues. Unlike the code reviewer, this one includes Edit because fixing bugs requires modifying code. The prompt provides a clear workflow from diagnosis to verification.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: Debugger
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：A subagent that can both analyze and fix issues. Unlike the code reviewer, this one includes Edit because fixing bugs requires modifying code. The prompt provides a clear workflow from diagnosis to verification. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: debugger
description: Debugging specialist for errors, test failures, and unexpected behavior. Use proactively when encountering any issues.
tools: Read, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob
---

You are an expert debugger specializing in root cause analysis.

When invoked:
1. Capture error message and stack trace
2. Identify reproduction steps
3. Isolate the failure location
4. Implement minimal fix
5. Verify solution works

Debugging process:
- Analyze error messages and logs
- Check recent code changes
- Form and test hypotheses
- Add strategic debug logging
- Inspect variable states

For each issue, provide:
- Root cause explanation
- Evidence supporting the diagnosis
- Specific code fix
- Testing approach
- Prevention recommendations

Focus on fixing the underlying issue, not the symptoms.

paragraphs: 这一节讲“Data scientist”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

这里还牵扯模型怎么选，速度、成本和能力通常要一起掂量。

steps.title: Data scientist
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：A domain-specific subagent for data analysis work. This example shows how to create subagents for specialized workflows outside of typical coding tasks. It explicitly sets model: sonnet for more capable analysis. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: data-scientist
description: Data analysis expert for SQL queries, BigQuery operations, and data insights. Use proactively for data analysis tasks and queries.
tools: Bash, Read, Write
model: sonnet
---

You are a data scientist specializing in SQL and BigQuery analysis.

When invoked:
1. Understand the data analysis requirement
2. Write efficient SQL queries
3. Use BigQuery command line tools (bq) when appropriate
4. Analyze and summarize results
5. Present findings clearly

Key practices:
- Write optimized SQL queries with proper filters
- Use appropriate aggregations and joins
- Include comments explaining complex logic
- Format results for readability
- Provide data-driven recommendations

For each analysis:
- Explain the query approach
- Document any assumptions
- Highlight key findings
- Suggest next steps based on data

Always ensure queries are efficient and cost-effective.

sections.title: Database query validator
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Database query validator”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Create the validation script anywhere in your project. The path must match the command field in your hook configuration:；Make the script executable:。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: Database query validator 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：A subagent that allows Bash access but validates commands to permit only read-only SQL queries. This example shows how to use PreToolUse hooks for conditional validation when you need finer control than the tools field provides. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: db-reader
description: Execute read-only database queries. Use when analyzing data or generating reports.
tools: Bash
hooks:
  PreToolUse:
    - matcher: "Bash"
      hooks:
        - type: command
          command: "./scripts/validate-readonly-query.sh"
---

You are a database analyst with read-only access. Execute SELECT queries to answer questions about the data.

When asked to analyze data:
1. Identify which tables contain the relevant data
2. Write efficient SELECT queries with appropriate filters
3. Present results clearly with context

You cannot modify data. If asked to INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or modify schema, explain that you only have read access.

steps.title: Database query validator 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：A subagent that allows Bash access but validates commands to permit only read-only SQL queries. This example shows how to use PreToolUse hooks for conditional validation when you need finer control than the tools field provides. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Blocks SQL write operations, allows SELECT queries

# Read JSON input from stdin
INPUT=$(cat)

# Extract the command field from tool_input using jq
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty')

if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

# Block write operations (case-insensitive)
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -iE '\b(INSERT|UPDATE|DELETE|DROP|CREATE|ALTER|TRUNCATE|REPLACE|MERGE)\b' > /dev/null; then
  echo "Blocked: Write operations not allowed. Use SELECT queries only." >&2
  exit 2
fi

exit 0

steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：A subagent that allows Bash access but validates commands to permit only read-only SQL queries. This example shows how to use PreToolUse hooks for conditional validation when you need finer control than the tools field provides. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

agent-teams.sourceLabel: Orchestrate teams of Claude Code sessions

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲代理团队，也就是让多名 Claude 同时协作。
适合任务大、分工明确、需要并行推进的场景。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这比子代理更像真施工队，不是一个人换帽子，而是几个人同时上工。
这一页会讲什么时候该用团队、它和子代理有啥区别、怎么开团队、怎么控制队员。
如果你的任务已经大到“一个人来回切角色很累”，团队模式就有价值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先确认任务真能拆开，再拉团队，不然只会多出协调成本。
最稳的分法一般是勘察、实现、验收三类人分开。
一开始团队别拉太大，先两三名角色试顺，再扩大。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Run a parallel code review
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：A single reviewer tends to gravitate toward one type of issue at a time. Splitting review criteria into independent domains means security, performance, and test coverage all get thorough attention simultaneously. The prompt assigns each teammate a distinct lens so they don’t overlap: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

Create an agent team to review PR #142. Spawn three reviewers:
- One focused on security implications
- One checking performance impact
- One validating test coverage
Have them each review and report findings.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Investigate with competing hypotheses
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：When the root cause is unclear, a single agent tends to find one plausible explanation and stop looking. The prompt fights this by making teammates explicitly adversarial: each one’s job is not only to investigate its own theory but to challenge the others’. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Users report the app exits after one message instead of staying connected.
Spawn 5 agent teammates to investigate different hypotheses. Have them talk to
each other to try to disprove each other's theories, like a scientific
debate. Update the findings doc with whatever consensus emerges.

at src/auth/ for security vulnerabilities. Focus on token handling, session
management, and input validation. The app uses JWT tokens stored in
httpOnly cookies. Report any issues with severity ratings."

sections.title: When to use agent teams
paragraphs: 这一节讲“When to use agent teams”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：New modules or features: teammates can each own a separate piece without stepping on each other；Debugging with competing hypotheses: teammates test different theories in parallel and converge on the answer faster；Cross-layer coordination: changes that span frontend, backend, and tests, each owned by a different teammate。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Compare with subagents
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Compare with subagents”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Both agent teams and subagents let you parallelize work, but they operate differently. Choose based on whether your workers need to communicate with each other:；Use subagents when you need quick, focused workers that report back. Use agent teams when teammates need to share findings, challenge each other, and coordinate on their own.。

sections.title: Require plan approval for teammates
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Require plan approval for teammates”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：For complex or risky tasks, you can require teammates to plan before implementing. The teammate works in read-only plan mode until the lead approves their approach:；When a teammate finishes planning, it sends a plan approval request to the lead. The lead reviews the plan and either approves it or rejects it with feedback. If rejected, the teammate stays in plan mode, revises based on the feedback, and resubmits. Once approved, the teammate exits plan mode and begins implementation.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Talk to teammates directly
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Talk to teammates directly”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Each teammate is a full, independent Claude Code session. You can message any teammate directly to give additional instructions, ask follow-up questions, or redirect their approach.；In-process mode: use Shift+Down to cycle through teammates, then type to send them a message. Press Enter to view a teammate’s session, then Escape to interrupt their current turn. Press Ctrl+T to toggle the task list.。

sections.title: Token usage
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Token usage”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Agent teams use significantly more tokens than a single session. Each teammate has its own context window, and token usage scales with the number of active teammates. For research, review, and new feature work, the extra tokens are usually worthwhile. For routine tasks, a single session is more cost-effective. See agent team token costs for usage guidance.。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：These examples show how agent teams handle tasks where parallel exploration adds value.。

sections.title: Run a parallel code review
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Run a parallel code review”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：A single reviewer tends to gravitate toward one type of issue at a time. Splitting review criteria into independent domains means security, performance, and test coverage all get thorough attention simultaneously. The prompt assigns each teammate a distinct lens so they don’t overlap:；Each reviewer works from the same PR but applies a different filter. The lead synthesizes findings across all three after they finish.。

steps.title: Run a parallel code review
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：A single reviewer tends to gravitate toward one type of issue at a time. Splitting review criteria into independent domains means security, performance, and test coverage all get thorough attention simultaneously. The prompt assigns each teammate a distinct lens so they don’t overlap: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Create an agent team to review PR #142. Spawn three reviewers:
- One focused on security implications
- One checking performance impact
- One validating test coverage
Have them each review and report findings.

sections.title: Investigate with competing hypotheses
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Investigate with competing hypotheses”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：When the root cause is unclear, a single agent tends to find one plausible explanation and stop looking. The prompt fights this by making teammates explicitly adversarial: each one’s job is not only to investigate its own theory but to challenge the others’.；The debate structure is the key mechanism here. Sequential investigation suffers from anchoring: once one theory is explored, subsequent investigation is biased toward it.。

steps.title: Investigate with competing hypotheses
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：When the root cause is unclear, a single agent tends to find one plausible explanation and stop looking. The prompt fights this by making teammates explicitly adversarial: each one’s job is not only to investigate its own theory but to challenge the others’. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Users report the app exits after one message instead of staying connected.
Spawn 5 agent teammates to investigate different hypotheses. Have them talk to
each other to try to disprove each other's theories, like a scientific
debate. Update the findings doc with whatever consensus emerges.

sections.title: Choose an appropriate team size
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Choose an appropriate team size”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：There’s no hard limit on the number of teammates, but practical constraints apply:；Coordination overhead increases: more teammates means more communication, task coordination, and potential for conflicts；Diminishing returns: beyond a certain point, additional teammates don’t speed up work proportionally。

sections.title: Size tasks appropriately
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Size tasks appropriately”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Too small: coordination overhead exceeds the benefit；Too large: teammates work too long without check-ins, increasing risk of wasted effort；Just right: self-contained units that produce a clear deliverable, such as a function, a test file, or a review。

sections.title: Start with research and review
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Start with research and review”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：If you’re new to agent teams, start with tasks that have clear boundaries and don’t require writing code: reviewing a PR, researching a library, or investigating a bug. These tasks show the value of parallel exploration without the coordination challenges that come with parallel implementation.。

sections.title: Avoid file conflicts
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Avoid file conflicts”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Two teammates editing the same file leads to overwrites. Break the work so each teammate owns a different set of files.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: Monitor and steer
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Monitor and steer”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Check in on teammates’ progress, redirect approaches that aren’t working, and synthesize findings as they come in. Letting a team run unattended for too long increases the risk of wasted effort.。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Build with Claude Code 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲怎么做自己的插件，把技能、代理、hooks、MCP 这些打成包。
做出来以后，别的项目和别人装上就能直接用。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 插件像打包好的工具箱，不用每换一块地就重新配一遍。
这一页会讲什么时候该做插件、什么时候只做独立配置就够、以及第一个插件怎么起步。
如果你们团队总在重复配同一套东西，这页很值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先确定你打包的是一类稳定能力，而不是一堆临时想法。
从最小插件结构做起，先让它能装、能跑，再慢慢加复杂内容。
技能、hooks、代理、MCP 往里放时，先想好谁是核心，不要把插件做成杂货铺。

name: code-review

discover-plugins.sourceLabel: Discover and install prebuilt plugins through marketplaces

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲去哪找现成插件、怎么装、装完能多出哪些本事。
重点不是装得多，而是装得准。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像去农具市场挑现成家伙。别人已经打磨好的，拿来就能省你很多重复造轮子的劲。
这一页还会讲市场怎么运作、官方市场里有什么、代码智能插件和外部集成插件能带来什么。
如果你懒得自己从零做插件，这页就是最省力的入口。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先装最贴近你当前活路的插件，别为了新鲜一口气装一堆。
看插件时，除了功能，也要看它到底会给 Claude 多开哪些能力。
装完先在小项目试，再决定要不要推广到主项目和团队里。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Build with Claude Code 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲技能，也就是把常用绝活做成随叫随到的小抄。
适合把团队里重复用的套路固化下来。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 技能像写在墙上的拿手活说明：遇到这种事，就按这个打法来。
这一页会讲内置技能、怎么做第一个技能、技能放哪儿、怎么自动发现。
如果你老是在反复教 Claude 同一种套路，这页就是答案。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 技能要短、准、能执行，不要写成一篇空洞演讲稿。
先从一个高频场景试，比如写测试、审查代码、生成文档。
目录结构和发现规则先弄清，不然写了半天找不着。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create your first skill 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：This example creates a skill that teaches Claude to explain code using visual diagrams and analogies. Since it uses default frontmatter, Claude can load it automatically when you ask how something works, or you can invoke it directly with /explain-code. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create your first skill 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：This example creates a skill that teaches Claude to explain code using visual diagrams and analogies. Since it uses default frontmatter, Claude can load it automatically when you ask how something works, or you can invoke it directly with /explain-code. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: explain-code
description: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
---

When explaining code, always include:

1. **Start with an analogy**: Compare the code to something from everyday life
2. **Draw a diagram**: Use ASCII art to show the flow, structure, or relationships
3. **Walk through the code**: Explain step-by-step what happens
4. **Highlight a gotcha**: What's a common mistake or misconception?

Keep explanations conversational. For complex concepts, use multiple analogies.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create your first skill 3
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：This example creates a skill that teaches Claude to explain code using visual diagrams and analogies. Since it uses default frontmatter, Claude can load it automatically when you ask how something works, or you can invoke it directly with /explain-code. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create your first skill 4

Since it uses default frontmatter, Claude can load it automatically when you ask how something works, or you can invoke it directly with /explain-code. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Where skills live
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一小节原文主要在讲：Where you store a skill determines who can use it: 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
my-skill/
├── SKILL.md           # Main instructions (required)
├── template.md        # Template for Claude to fill in
├── examples/
│   └── sample.md      # Example output showing expected format
└── scripts/
    └── validate.sh    # Script Claude can execute

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Types of skill content 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Skill files can contain any instructions, but thinking about how you want to invoke them helps guide what to include: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: api-conventions
description: API design patterns for this codebase
---

When writing API endpoints:
- Use RESTful naming conventions
- Return consistent error formats
- Include request validation

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Types of skill content 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Skill files can contain any instructions, but thinking about how you want to invoke them helps guide what to include: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: deploy
description: Deploy the application to production
context: fork
disable-model-invocation: true
---

Deploy the application:
1. Run the test suite
2. Build the application
3. Push to the deployment target

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Frontmatter reference 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Beyond the markdown content, you can configure skill behavior using YAML frontmatter fields between --- markers at the top of your SKILL.md file: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: my-skill
description: What this skill does
disable-model-invocation: true
allowed-tools: Read, Grep
---

Your skill instructions here...

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Frontmatter reference 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Beyond the markdown content, you can configure skill behavior using YAML frontmatter fields between --- markers at the top of your SKILL.md file: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: session-logger
description: Log activity for this session
---

Log the following to logs/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}.log:

$ARGUMENTS

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Add supporting files 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一小节原文主要在讲：Skills can include multiple files in their directory. This keeps SKILL.md focused on the essentials while letting Claude access detailed reference material only when needed. Large reference docs, API specifications, or example collections don’t need to load into context every time the skill runs. 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
my-skill/
├── SKILL.md (required - overview and navigation)
├── reference.md (detailed API docs - loaded when needed)
├── examples.md (usage examples - loaded when needed)
└── scripts/
    └── helper.py (utility script - executed, not loaded)

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Add supporting files 2
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Skills can include multiple files in their directory. This keeps SKILL.md focused on the essentials while letting Claude access detailed reference material only when needed. Large reference docs, API specifications, or example collections don’t need to load into context every time the skill runs. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
## Additional resources

- For complete API details, see [reference.md](reference.md)
- For usage examples, see [examples.md](examples.md)

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Control who invokes a skill
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：By default, both you and Claude can invoke any skill. You can type /skill-name to invoke it directly, and Claude can load it automatically when relevant to your conversation. Two frontmatter fields let you restrict this: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: deploy
description: Deploy the application to production
disable-model-invocation: true
---

Deploy $ARGUMENTS to production:

1. Run the test suite
2. Build the application
3. Push to the deployment target
4. Verify the deployment succeeded

sections.title: Bundled skills
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Bundled skills”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Bundled skills ship with Claude Code and are available in every session. Unlike built-in commands, which execute fixed logic directly, bundled skills are prompt-based: they give Claude a detailed playbook and let it orchestrate the work using its tools. This means bundled skills can spawn parallel agents, read files, and adapt to your codebase.；You invoke bundled skills the same way as any other skill: type / followed by the skill name. In the table below, <arg> indicates a required argument and [arg] indicates an optional one.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

2. **Draw a diagram**: Use ASCII art to show the flow, structure, or relationships
3. **Walk through the code**: Explain step-by-step what happens
4. **Highlight a gotcha**: What's a common mistake or misconception?

Keep explanations conversational. For complex concepts, use multiple analogies.

One powerful pattern is generating visual output: interactive HTML files that open in your browser for exploring data, debugging, or creating reports. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Skills can bundle and run scripts in any language, giving Claude capabilities beyond what’s possible in a single prompt. One powerful pattern is generating visual output: interactive HTML files that open in your browser for exploring data, debugging, or creating reports. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: codebase-visualizer
description: Generate an interactive collapsible tree visualization of your codebase. Use when exploring a new repo, understanding project structure, or identifying large files.
allowed-tools: Bash(python *)
---

# Codebase Visualizer

Generate an interactive HTML tree view that shows your project's file structure with collapsible directories.

## Usage

Run the visualization script from your project root:

```bash
python ~/.claude/skills/codebase-visualizer/scripts/visualize.py .
```text

This creates `codebase-map.html` in the current directory and opens it in your default browser.

## What the visualization shows

- **Collapsible directories**: Click folders to expand/collapse
- **File sizes**: Displayed next to each file
- **Colors**: Different colors for different file types
- **Directory totals**: Shows aggregate size of each folder

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲定时任务，让 Claude 到点自动跑提示。
适合循环提醒、轮询状态、固定时间做一遍检查。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像你让帮工每天早上先去看一眼水泵，每周一再盘一次仓库。
这一页会讲 `/loop` 怎么用、间隔语法、一次性提醒、任务怎么管理。
如果你总有重复催一次的事，这页特别省心。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从最简单的重复活开始，不要一开始就排一大串复杂任务。
循环间隔和到期逻辑要看清，别设完就不管。
任务真跑起来后，要定期回头看有没有空转或误报。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Schedule a recurring prompt with /loop
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：The /loop bundled skill is the quickest way to schedule a recurring prompt. Pass an optional interval and a prompt, and Claude sets up a cron job that fires in the background while the session stays open. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: How scheduled tasks run
paragraphs: 这一节讲“How scheduled tasks run”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：The scheduler checks every second for due tasks and enqueues them at low priority. A scheduled prompt fires between your turns, not while Claude is mid-response. If Claude is busy when a task comes due, the prompt waits until the current turn ends.；All times are interpreted in your local timezone. A cron expression like 0 9 * * * means 9am wherever you’re running Claude Code, not UTC.。

sections.title: Jitter
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Jitter”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：One-shot tasks scheduled for the top or bottom of the hour fire up to 90 seconds early.。
这部分还会反复提作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

sections.title: Three-day expiry
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Three-day expiry”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Recurring tasks automatically expire 3 days after creation. The task fires one final time, then deletes itself. This bounds how long a forgotten loop can run. If you need a recurring task to last longer, cancel and recreate it before it expires, or use Desktop scheduled tasks for durable scheduling.。

sections.title: Cron expression reference
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Cron expression reference”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：CronCreate accepts standard 5-field cron expressions: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week. All fields support wildcards (*), single values (5), steps (*/15), ranges (1-5), and comma-separated lists (1,15,30).；Day-of-week uses 0 or 7 for Sunday through 6 for Saturday. Extended syntax like L, W, ?, and name aliases such as MON or JAN is not supported.。

sections.title: Disable scheduled tasks
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Disable scheduled tasks”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Set CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON=1 in your environment to disable the scheduler entirely. The cron tools and /loop become unavailable, and any already-scheduled tasks stop firing. See Environment variables for the full list of disable flags.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲输出风格，意思是 Claude 最后把结果说成什么样。
不只是写代码时能用，做摘要、报告、清单、结构化输出时也很有用。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把它想成给帮工提前定口径。是让它写成清单、写成报告、写成像客服话术，还是写成结构化格式，先说好，后面省很多返工。
这一页特别实用，因为很多时候问题不在 Claude 不会，而在它说出来的样子不合用。
如果你想把 Claude 从“程序员帮手”扩到更多工作场景，这页很值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先用内置输出风格试手，确认哪种最适合你的活。
真要自定义时，先把格式目标讲清楚，不要又想自由发挥又想字段固定。
这页最好和 `CLAUDE.md`、agents、append-system-prompt 一起分清楚谁管什么。

sections.title: Output Styles vs. CLAUDE.md vs. —append-system-prompt
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Output Styles vs. CLAUDE.md vs. —append-system-prompt”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Output styles completely “turn off” the parts of Claude Code’s default system prompt specific to software engineering. Neither CLAUDE.md nor --append-system-prompt edit Claude Code’s default system prompt. CLAUDE.md adds the contents as a user message following Claude Code’s default system prompt. --append-system-prompt appends the content to the system prompt.。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 hooks，也就是在特定时刻自动插动作。
改完文件自动格式化、任务结束发提醒、碰到危险命令先拦一下，都属于这类。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: Hooks 像门口的检查员和工地边上的规矩牌。事情发生到某一步，就自动触发。
这一页会讲第一个 hook 怎么配、能自动化什么、怎么提醒输入、怎么改完就自动格式化。
如果你想把规矩落地，不想每次靠人记，这页很好用。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从最稳的 hook 开始，比如格式化、通知、阻止改受保护文件。
触发时机一定要分清，不然 hook 一多就容易互相绊。
写 hook 时别贪重，越复杂越难维护。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Block edits to protected files 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Prevent Claude from modifying sensitive files like .env, package-lock.json, or anything in .git/. Claude receives feedback explaining why the edit was blocked, so it can adjust its approach. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
#!/bin/bash
# protect-files.sh

INPUT=$(cat)
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty')

PROTECTED_PATTERNS=(".env" "package-lock.json" ".git/")

for pattern in "${PROTECTED_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
  if [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"$pattern"* ]]; then
    echo "Blocked: $FILE_PATH matches protected pattern '$pattern'" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
done

exit 0

sections.title: Block edits to protected files
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Block edits to protected files”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Create the hook script；Save this to .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh:；Make the script executable (macOS/Linux)。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: Block edits to protected files 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Prevent Claude from modifying sensitive files like .env, package-lock.json, or anything in .git/. Claude receives feedback explaining why the edit was blocked, so it can adjust its approach. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
#!/bin/bash
# protect-files.sh

INPUT=$(cat)
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty')

PROTECTED_PATTERNS=(".env" "package-lock.json" ".git/")

for pattern in "${PROTECTED_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
  if [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"$pattern"* ]]; then
    echo "Blocked: $FILE_PATH matches protected pattern '$pattern'" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
done

exit 0

paragraphs: 这一节讲“Read input and return output”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。
原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：The exit code determines what happens next:；Exit 2: the action is blocked. Write a reason to stderr, and Claude receives it as feedback so it can adjust.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

steps.title: Read input and return output 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Hooks communicate with Claude Code through stdin, stdout, stderr, and exit codes. When an event fires, Claude Code passes event-specific data as JSON to your script’s stdin. Your script reads that data, does its work, and tells Claude Code what to do next via the exit code. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Read input and return output 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：Hooks communicate with Claude Code through stdin, stdout, stderr, and exit codes. When an event fires, Claude Code passes event-specific data as JSON to your script’s stdin. Your script reads that data, does its work, and tells Claude Code what to do next via the exit code. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
#!/bin/bash
INPUT=$(cat)
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command')

if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -q "drop table"; then
  echo "Blocked: dropping tables is not allowed" >&2  # stderr becomes Claude's feedback
  exit 2                                               # exit 2 = block the action
fi

exit 0  # exit 0 = let it proceed

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：The model’s only job is to return a yes/no decision as JSON:；"ok": true: the action proceeds；"ok": false: the action is blocked. The model’s "reason" is fed back to Claude so it can adjust.。
这里还牵扯模型怎么选，速度、成本和能力通常要一起掂量。

steps.title: Prompt-based hooks
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：For decisions that require judgment rather than deterministic rules, use type: "prompt" hooks. Instead of running a shell command, Claude Code sends your prompt and the hook’s input data to a Claude model (Haiku by default) to make the decision. You can specify a different model with the model field if you need more capability. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Limitations
paragraphs: 这一节讲“Limitations”。先把这小块的门道听明白，再照着下面的命令和配置去做。

原文这段重点会围着这些事打转：Hook timeout is 10 minutes by default, configurable per hook with the timeout field (in seconds).；PostToolUse hooks cannot undo actions since the tool has already executed.；Stop hooks fire whenever Claude finishes responding, not only at task completion. They do not fire on user interrupts.。
这一段尤其要盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。
